response.justification: Strong individual-contributor breadth with relevant developer-tooling, CI/CD, and testing exposure, plus teaching/workshop experience that hints at leadership ability. However, the resume shows no explicit people-management or direct-report experience, no formal tech-lead title, and only dated, limited mobile-platform work — all central to this Engineering Manager role. Worth a screening call to probe management scope, but not a clear fit on paper.
strengths: Deep multi-decade engineering background across web, mobile, and embedded with TDD/craftsmanship focus
Hands-on developer-tooling and infrastructure work (MCP generators, CI/CD, GitLab, Docker, test frameworks like Trial and Fluent-Asserts)
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing via workshops, courses, and conference talks — a proxy signal for team development skill
concerns: No explicit people-management experience or direct-report leadership shown; titles are all IC ('Senior/Staff Developer')
Mobile-platform experience is thin and dated (iOS/Apple TV work circa 2001–2002), weak for a role supporting a major mobile ecosystem
Resume contains overlapping date ranges and inconsistencies that raise credibility questions and need clarification

key_factors.factor: Lack of people management track record
Developer tooling and testing infrastructure depth
